title: Genesis: AgentScribe Implementation
description:
## Genesis Bead
Tied to plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md

## Overview
AgentScribe is a Rust CLI binary that scrapes conversation logs from multiple coding agent types (Claude Code, Aider, OpenCode, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf), normalizes them into a canonical format, stores them as flat files with a Tantivy search index, and provides a query interface for agents seeking past solutions.

## Progress
- [x] Phase 1: Plugin System, Scraping & Normalization (agentscribe-mkg) — COMPLETE
- [ ] Phase 2: Tantivy Indexing & Search (agentscribe-2dp.1) — IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Phase 3: Enrichment & Intelligence — pending
- [ ] Phase 4: Daemon Mode & MCP — pending
- [ ] Phase 5: SQLite Format Support & Extended Agents — pending
- [ ] Phase 6: Analytics, Knowledge Synthesis & Shell Integration — pending

## Status
Phase 1 complete. Phase 2 bead created and unblocked. Phases 3-6 beads to be created as Phase 2 completes.

title: Phase 2: Tantivy Indexing & Search
description:
## Phase 2: Tantivy Indexing & Search

### Objectives
- Build Tantivy index from normalized sessions (BM25 scoring, faceted fields)
- Implement incremental indexing on scrape (no full rebuild required)
- Build three-tier tag extraction pipeline (explicit, structural, keyword list)
- Implement fuzzy search via Tantivy's Levenshtein term queries
- Implement "more like this" search via Tantivy's MoreLikeThisQuery
- Build context budget packing with greedy knapsack algorithm
- Implement structured JSON output mode for agent consumption

### CLI Commands
- `agentscribe index rebuild|stats|optimize` — manage the Tantivy search index
- `agentscribe search` — query interface (primary interface for agents)
- `agentscribe status` — show tracked agents, session counts, daemon state

### Tantivy Index Schema
See plan.md lines 347-391 for the full schema definition. Key fields:
- Full-text: `content`, `summary`, `solution_summary`, `code_content`
- Faceted: `session_id`, `source_agent`, `project`, `tags`, `outcome`, `error_fingerprint`, `file_paths`, `git_commits`, `doc_type`
- Code artifacts: `code_language`, `code_file_path`, `code_is_final`
- Date/numeric: `timestamp`, `turn_count`

### Tag Extraction Pipeline (Three-Tier)
1. **Explicit:** tool names from `tool_call` events (`Edit`, `Bash`, `Read`), languages from code fence markers (`` ```rust `` → `rust`)
2. **Structural:** file extensions from `file_paths` (`.rs` → `rust`, `.py` → `python`), command names from Bash content (`docker`, `git`, `npm`, `cargo`, `kubectl`), error types from `error_fingerprints`
3. **Keyword list:** match content against bundled technology dictionary (~200 terms: frameworks, databases, cloud services, protocols). Exact word-boundary matches only.

### Search Features
- Full-text BM25 search across session content and summaries (default mode)
- Error lookup: `--error <pattern>` against normalized error fingerprints
- Code search: `--code <query> [--lang <lang>]` for code artifacts
- Solution-only: `--solution-only` to return only extracted solutions
- Similar sessions: `--like <session-id>` using Tantivy MoreLikeThis
- Filters: `--agent`, `--project`, `--since`/`--before`, `--tag`, `--outcome`, `--type`, `--model`
- Output: `--max-results`, `--snippet-length`, `--token-budget <n>` (greedy knapsack)
- Structured: `--json` for agent consumption

### Content Truncation Policy
- `user` and `assistant` content: indexed in full
- `tool_call` content: indexed in full
- `tool_result` content: truncated to first 1000 chars per event
- Total document content capped at 500KB (trim middle if exceeded: keep first 250KB + last 250KB)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tantivy index creation with full schema (all fields from plan.md)
- [ ] One document per session, concatenated with role prefixes
- [ ] Content truncation policy implemented (1000 chars for tool_result, 500KB total cap)
- [ ] Incremental indexing via `IndexWriter::add_document()` on scrape
- [ ] `agentscribe index rebuild` recreates index from session files
- [ ] `agentscribe index stats` shows document count, index size, segment info
- [ ] `agentscribe index optimize` compacts segments
- [ ] Three-tier tag extraction pipeline (explicit, structural, keyword list)
- [ ] Bundled technology keyword dictionary (~200 terms)
- [ ] Full-text BM25 search with default session-only results
- [ ] Fuzzy search via Levenshtein term queries
- [ ] `--like <session-id>` MoreLikeThis search
- [ ] `--error <pattern>` error fingerprint search
- [ ] `--code <query> [--lang <lang>]` code artifact search
- [ ] `--solution-only` filter
- [ ] All filter flags: `--agent`, `--project`, `--since`/`--before`, `--tag`, `--outcome`, `--type`, `--model`
- [ ] `--json` structured output mode
- [ ] `--token-budget <n>` greedy knapsack context packing
- [ ] `--max-results` and `--snippet-length` basic sizing
- [ ] `agentscribe status` command
- [ ] doc_type facet filtering (sessions vs code_artifacts, never mixed)

### References
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md (lines 347-391 for index schema, lines 412-423 for phase scope, lines 465-510 for search interface)
- CLI reference: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/cli-reference.md
- Phase 1 bead: agentscribe-mkg

title: Phase 6: Analytics, Knowledge Synthesis & Shell Integration
description:
Implement analytics, knowledge synthesis, and shell integration features.

## Deliverables

### Recurring Problem Detection
- Group error fingerprints by frequency (GROUP BY fingerprint HAVING count >= 3)
- `agentscribe recurring [--since <date>] [--threshold <n>]` command
- Output: frequency, affected projects, which agents solved it, last fix
- Files: src/analytics/recurring.rs, src/cli/recurring.rs

### Agent Effectiveness Analytics
- Success rate per agent, average turns/tokens per resolution
- Problem type classification (debug/feature/refactor/investigation/configuration/documentation)
- Cost efficiency metrics using [cost.models] from config.toml
- Trend analysis over time
- `agentscribe analytics [--agent <name>] [--project <path>] [--since <date>]`
- Files: src/analytics/effectiveness.rs, src/cli/analytics.rs

### Auto-Generated Project Rules
- Extract user corrections, tool preferences, architecture conventions
- Output formats: --format claude (CLAUDE.md), --format cursor (.cursorrules), --format aider (.aider.conf.yml)
- `agentscribe rules <project-path> [--format claude|cursor|aider]`
- Files: src/analytics/rules.rs, src/cli/rules.rs

### File Knowledge Map Enhancements
- Add "known gotchas" section from anti-patterns filtered to the file
- Files: src/analytics/file_knowledge.rs

### Weekly Digest
- `agentscribe digest [--since 7d] [--output <path>]`
- Sessions completed, problems solved, recurring issues, agent comparison
- Most-touched files, token usage trends
- Markdown output suitable for 2-minute skim
- Files: src/analytics/digest.rs, src/cli/digest.rs

### Search-on-Error Shell Hook
- `agentscribe shell-hook bash|zsh|fish` generates shell integration
- PROMPT_COMMAND/precmd hook detects non-zero exit, queries fingerprint index
- Background subprocess, never blocks shell
- Files: src/cli/shell_hook.rs, templates/shell_hook_{bash,zsh,fish}.sh

### Session Lifecycle Management (GC)
- `agentscribe gc [--older-than <duration>] [--dry-run]`
- Delete normalized session files, remove Tantivy documents, compact index
- Respect max_session_age_days from config.toml
- Files: src/cli/gc.rs

## Testing
- Test recurring detection with synthetic fingerprint data
- Test analytics accuracy with known session outcomes
- Test shell hook generation for all three shells
- Test gc --dry-run shows deletions without acting
- Test rules generation produces valid CLAUDE.md/cursorrules/aider.conf.yml

## Acceptance Criteria
- `agentscribe recurring` surfaces problems solved 3+ times in the window
- `agentscribe analytics` shows per-agent success rates and cost efficiency
- `agentscribe rules` generates valid agent-specific rules files
- `agentscribe digest --since 7d` produces readable Markdown summary
- Shell hook triggers background search on command failure without blocking
- `agentscribe gc --older-than 90d` correctly removes old sessions and recompacts index

title: Tag extraction: keyword dictionary and integration
description:
## Tag Extraction: Keyword Dictionary and Integration

Implement tier 3 (keyword dictionary matching) and integrate all three tiers into the document builder.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Modify `src/tags.rs` — add keyword dictionary and top-level extract function
- Create `data/tech_keywords.txt` — bundled keyword dictionary (~200 terms, one per line)
- Modify `src/index.rs` — call extract_tags during document building and populate tags facet

### What to Implement
- `data/tech_keywords.txt`: ~200 technology terms (frameworks: react, vue, django, flask, express, rails; databases: postgres, redis, mongodb, mysql, sqlite; cloud: aws, gcp, azure, s3, lambda, cloudflare; protocols: grpc, graphql, rest, websocket; tools: terraform, ansible, kubernetes, helm, docker; languages: not needed, covered by tiers 1-2)
- `pub fn extract_keyword_tags(content: &str, dictionary: &[String]) -> Vec<String>`
  - Word-boundary regex match only (no substring matches — "react" should not match "reactive")
  - Case-insensitive matching
  - Load dictionary once at startup (lazy_static or OnceCell)
- `pub fn extract_tags(events: &[CanonicalEvent]) -> Vec<String>` — combines all three tiers, deduplicates, sorts
- Integration: call `extract_tags` in `build_document()` and populate the `tags` facet field

### Test Command
```bash
cargo test --lib tags::tests && test -f data/tech_keywords.txt && wc -l data/tech_keywords.txt | awk '{if ($1 >= 150) exit 0; else exit 1}'
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- Unit test: "using react and postgres" matches tags ["react", "postgres"] but NOT "reactive"
- Unit test: word boundary — "rediscover" does NOT produce tag "redis"
- Unit test: case insensitive — "Docker" matches "docker"
- Unit test: combined extract_tags merges all three tiers without duplicates
- data/tech_keywords.txt exists with at least 150 terms
- Integration: build_document produces a Document with populated tags facet

title: Document builder: session-to-document conversion
description:
## Document Builder: Session-to-Document Conversion

Implement the document builder that converts normalized sessions into Tantivy documents. Second half of agentscribe-y71.

### Files to Modify
- `src/index.rs` — add document builder functions

### What to Implement
- `pub fn build_document(schema: &Schema, session: &NormalizedSession) -> tantivy::Document`
  - Concatenates conversation turns with role prefixes ("user: ...", "assistant: ...") into `content` field
  - Populates all faceted fields from session metadata
  - Sets `timestamp`, `turn_count`
  - Sets `doc_type` facet to "sessions"
- `pub fn build_code_artifact_doc(schema: &Schema, artifact: &CodeArtifact) -> tantivy::Document`
  - Sets `code_content`, `code_language`, `code_file_path`, `code_is_final`
  - Sets `doc_type` facet to "code_artifacts"
- Content truncation policy:
  - `tool_result` content capped at 1000 characters
  - Total `content` field capped at 500KB; if over, trim middle with "[...truncated...]" marker

### Test Command
```bash
cargo test --lib index::tests::build_document
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- Unit test: build_document with a 3-turn session produces a Document with correct content field containing role prefixes
- Unit test: tool_result over 1000 chars is truncated to 1000
- Unit test: content over 500KB has middle trimmed with marker
- Unit test: all facet fields are populated from session metadata
- Unit test: build_code_artifact_doc sets doc_type to "code_artifacts"

title: Tag extraction: explicit and structural tiers
description:
## Tag Extraction: Explicit and Structural Tiers

Implement tiers 1 and 2 of the three-tier tag extraction pipeline.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create `src/tags.rs` — new module
- Modify `src/main.rs` — add `mod tags;` declaration

### What to Implement
- `pub fn extract_explicit_tags(events: &[CanonicalEvent]) -> Vec<String>`
  - Extract tool names from tool_call events (Edit, Bash, Read, Write, Grep, Glob)
  - Extract language names from code fence markers (```rust -> "rust", ```python -> "python", etc.)
  - Deduplicate and lowercase
- `pub fn extract_structural_tags(events: &[CanonicalEvent]) -> Vec<String>`
  - Map file extensions to languages from file_paths (.rs -> "rust", .py -> "python", .ts -> "typescript", .go -> "go", .jsx -> "react", etc.) — at least 15 extension mappings
  - Extract command names from Bash content (docker, git, npm, cargo, kubectl, make, pip) — word-boundary match on first token of command
  - Extract error type tags from error_fingerprints if present

### Test Command
```bash
cargo test --lib tags::tests
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- Unit test: tool_call event with tool="Edit" produces tag "edit"
- Unit test: code fence ```python produces tag "python"
- Unit test: file_path "src/main.rs" produces tag "rust"
- Unit test: Bash content "docker build ." produces tag "docker"
- Unit test: duplicate tags are deduplicated
- Unit test: all tags are lowercase
- At least 15 file extension -> language mappings defined

title: Tag extraction pipeline (three-tier)
description:
## Three-Tier Tag Extraction Pipeline

Build the tag extraction system that automatically tags sessions during indexing.

### Three Tiers
1. **Explicit:** Extract tool names from tool_call events (Edit, Bash, Read), languages from code fence markers (```rust -> "rust")
2. **Structural:** File extensions from file_paths (.rs -> "rust", .py -> "python"), command names from Bash content (docker, git, npm, cargo, kubectl), error types from error_fingerprints
3. **Keyword list:** Match content against bundled technology dictionary (~200 terms: frameworks, databases, cloud services, protocols). Exact word-boundary matches only.

### Deliverables
- src/tags.rs module with extract_tags(session_events) -> Vec<String>
- Bundled keyword dictionary file (~200 technology terms)
- Integration with document builder (tags populated at index time)
- Unit tests for each tier

### References
- Phase 2 bead: agentscribe-2dp.1
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md

title: Index management CLI commands (rebuild, stats, optimize)
description:
## Index Management CLI Commands

Implement the agentscribe index subcommand with rebuild, stats, and optimize operations.

### Commands
- agentscribe index rebuild — Recreate the entire Tantivy index from session files in ~/.agentscribe/sessions/
- agentscribe index stats — Show document count, index size on disk, segment count/info
- agentscribe index optimize — Compact segments (merge small segments into larger ones)

### Deliverables
- CLI subcommand registration in src/cli.rs
- Index rebuild: iterate all session .jsonl files, build documents, commit to fresh index
- Index stats: read index metadata, display in human-readable format
- Index optimize: call IndexWriter::merge on segments
- Incremental indexing hook: IndexWriter::add_document() called during scrape (integrate with existing scraper)
- Progress reporting during rebuild (session count, elapsed time)

### References
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md
- CLI reference: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/cli-reference.md

title: Phase 1: Plugin System, Scraping & Normalization
description:
## Phase 1: Plugin System, Scraping & Normalization

### Objectives
- Implement plugin system with TOML-based scraper definitions
- Build format-specific parsers (JSONL, Markdown, JSON tree)
- Implement session detection strategies (one-file-per-session, timestamp-gap, delimiter)
- Bundle default plugins for Claude Code, Aider, OpenCode, Codex
- Normalize all formats to canonical event schema
- Track scrape state for incremental scrapes
- Implement skip-and-log error handling at every level
- Extract file paths from events (structured + regex fallback)

### CLI Commands
-  - Initialize global config and data directory
-  - Discover and read agent log files
-  - List available plugins
-  - Validate plugin definitions
-  - Show plugin details

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Plugin TOML schema defined and documented
- [ ] JSONL parser with field mapping support
- [ ] Markdown parser with delimiter-based session detection
- [ ] JSON tree parser for OpenCode format
- [ ] Canonical event schema implementation
- [ ] Scrape state tracking (last-byte-offset, last-modified, session IDs)
- [ ] Incremental scraping (seek to offset on re-run)
- [ ] Error handling: skip malformed lines/fields/files with logging
- [ ] File path extraction from tool_call fields
- [ ] File path extraction via regex from content strings
- [ ] Bundled plugins: claude-code.toml, aider.toml, opencode.toml, codex.toml
- [ ] Data directory layout: config.toml, plugins/, sessions/, state/
- [ ] CLI commands functional

### References
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md
- Plugin authoring guide: /home/coding/AgentScribe/plugins/BUILDING_PLUGINS.md

title: Search command with BM25, filters, and output modes
description:
## Search Command Implementation

Implement the agentscribe search command — the primary query interface for agents and humans.

### Search Features
- Full-text BM25 search across session content and summaries (default mode)
- Fuzzy search via Tantivy Levenshtein term queries
- Error lookup: --error <pattern> against normalized error fingerprints
- Code search: --code <query> [--lang <lang>] for code artifacts
- Solution-only: --solution-only to return only extracted solutions
- Similar sessions: --like <session-id> using Tantivy MoreLikeThis

### Filter Flags
- --agent (source agent type)
- --project (project path)
- --since / --before (date range)
- --tag (extracted tags)
- --outcome (success/failure/abandoned/unknown)
- --type (doc_type: session or code_artifact)
- --model (model name)

### Output Modes
- Default: human-readable with snippets
- --json: structured JSON output for agent consumption
- --max-results N: limit result count
- --snippet-length N: control snippet size
- --token-budget N: greedy knapsack context packing (fit as many relevant results as possible within N tokens)

### Deliverables
- CLI subcommand in src/cli.rs
- src/search.rs module with query building, result formatting
- Greedy knapsack algorithm for --token-budget
- Unit tests for query construction and result formatting
- Integration test with a small test index

### References
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md (lines 465-510)
- CLI reference: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/cli-reference.md

title: Phase 4: Daemon Mode & MCP
description:
Implement the long-running daemon for continuous scraping and optional MCP server.

## Deliverables

### Daemon Core
- `agentscribe daemon start|stop|status|run|logs` commands
- File watcher using notify crate (inotify/fswatch) for automatic scraping
- Scrape debounce (default 5s) to avoid thrashing during active sessions
- Incremental index updates (no full rebuild per session)
- PID file management (~/.agentscribe/agentscribe.pid)
- Log file (~/.agentscribe/daemon.log)
- Files: src/daemon/mod.rs, src/daemon/watcher.rs, src/cli/daemon.rs

### MCP Server (Optional)
- Unix socket MCP server at ~/.agentscribe/mcp.sock
- Four tools: agentscribe_search, agentscribe_status, agentscribe_blame, agentscribe_file
- All tools call same core library as CLI
- Config toggle: [daemon] mcp_enabled = false
- Files: src/mcp/mod.rs, src/mcp/tools.rs

### Systemd Integration
- Generate systemd user-level service unit file
- `agentscribe daemon install` to create ~/.config/systemd/user/agentscribe.service
- Files: src/daemon/systemd.rs

## Testing
- Integration test: daemon watches a directory, detects new file, scrapes automatically
- Test debounce: rapid file changes produce single scrape
- Test MCP tools return same results as CLI equivalents
- Test daemon graceful shutdown (SIGTERM)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `agentscribe daemon start` runs in background, watches all plugin source paths
- New agent log files are automatically scraped within debounce window
- `agentscribe daemon status` shows PID, uptime, RSS, last scrape time
- MCP server (when enabled) responds to all four tool calls correctly
- Daemon RSS stays under 20MB when idle

title: Tantivy schema definition and index creation
description:
## Tantivy Schema Definition and Index Creation

Implement the Tantivy schema and index open/create logic. This is the first half of agentscribe-y71.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create `src/index.rs` — new module
- Modify `src/main.rs` — add `mod index;` declaration
- Modify `Cargo.toml` — add `tantivy` dependency

### What to Implement
- `pub fn build_schema() -> tantivy::schema::Schema` returning a schema with:
  - Full-text TEXT fields: `content`, `summary`, `solution_summary`, `code_content`
  - Faceted FACET fields: `session_id`, `source_agent`, `project`, `tags`, `outcome`, `error_fingerprint`, `file_paths`, `git_commits`, `doc_type`
  - Code artifact fields: `code_language` (FACET), `code_file_path` (FACET), `code_is_final` (U64)
  - Date field: `timestamp` (DATE)
  - Numeric field: `turn_count` (U64, INDEXED+STORED)
- `pub fn open_or_create_index(path: &Path, schema: &Schema) -> tantivy::Result<Index>` — creates dir if missing, opens existing index or creates new one

### Test Command
```bash
cargo test --lib index::tests
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- `build_schema()` returns a Schema with all 16+ fields named above
- `open_or_create_index` creates a new index in a temp dir and returns Ok
- `open_or_create_index` on an existing index dir returns Ok without error
- Unit test: round-trip — create schema, open index, verify all field names exist via `schema.get_field(name)`
- Unit test: index dir is created if it doesn't exist

title: Phase 3: Enrichment & Intelligence
description:
Implement the enrichment pipeline that adds intelligence to scraped sessions.

## Deliverables

### Summaries
- Auto-generate session summaries from first user prompt + outcome + files touched
- `agentscribe summarize` CLI command
- Files: src/enrichment/summary.rs, src/cli/summarize.rs

### Outcome Detection
- Signal-scoring system to classify sessions as success/failure/abandoned/unknown
- Configurable weights in config.toml under [outcome.weights]
- Files: src/enrichment/outcome.rs

### Solution Extraction
- Identify resolution window (last error → user confirmation)
- Extract Edit/Write/Bash tool calls as solution_summary
- Files: src/enrichment/solution.rs

### Error Fingerprinting
- Regex pipeline to extract and normalize error patterns
- Strip variable parts (paths, hosts, ports, PIDs, timestamps, UUIDs)
- Store as error_fingerprint facet in Tantivy
- Files: src/enrichment/errors.rs

### Anti-Pattern Detection
- Analyze failed/abandoned sessions for rejection windows
- Link to successful alternatives via error fingerprint matching
- Store in <session-id>.anti-patterns.jsonl sidecar files
- Files: src/enrichment/antipatterns.rs

### Code Artifact Extraction
- Extract fenced code blocks from assistant responses
- Index as separate Tantivy documents with doc_type: "code_artifact"
- Track language, file path, session ID, code_is_final flag
- Files: src/enrichment/code_artifacts.rs

### Git Commit Correlation
- `git log --after=<start> --before=<end>` during session windows
- Reverse index: commit_hash → session_id
- `agentscribe blame` CLI command
- `agentscribe file` CLI command
- Files: src/enrichment/git.rs, src/cli/blame.rs, src/cli/file.rs

## Testing
- Unit tests for each enrichment module
- Integration tests with sample session data
- Validate fingerprint normalization against known error patterns

## Acceptance Criteria
- `agentscribe summarize` generates accurate one-line summaries
- Outcome detection correctly classifies test sessions with known outcomes
- Error fingerprints match across sessions with same error types but different variable parts
- `agentscribe blame src/file.rs:42` links to the correct session
- Anti-patterns include working alternatives when available

title: Tantivy index schema and document builder
description:
## Tantivy Index Schema and Document Builder

Create the Tantivy index with the full schema and implement the document builder that converts normalized sessions into indexed documents.

### Deliverables
- Tantivy index creation with all fields from plan.md:
  - Full-text: content, summary, solution_summary, code_content
  - Faceted: session_id, source_agent, project, tags, outcome, error_fingerprint, file_paths, git_commits, doc_type
  - Code artifacts: code_language, code_file_path, code_is_final
  - Date/numeric: timestamp, turn_count
- Document builder: one document per session, content concatenated with role prefixes
- Content truncation policy: tool_result capped at 1000 chars, total 500KB (trim middle)
- doc_type facet (sessions vs code_artifacts)

### Implementation Notes
- Add tantivy dependency to Cargo.toml
- Create src/index.rs module
- Schema definition as a function returning tantivy::schema::Schema
- Document builder takes a Vec<CanonicalEvent> and produces a tantivy::Document

### References
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md (lines 347-391 for schema)
- Phase 2 bead: agentscribe-2dp.1

title: Status command
description:
## Status Command

Implement the agentscribe status command to show a summary of tracked agents, session counts, index health, and daemon state.

### Output
- Per-agent: name, session count, last scraped timestamp, total events
- Index: document count, size on disk, last rebuild time
- Daemon: running/stopped, PID, uptime (if applicable — daemon is Phase 4)
- Scrape state: number of tracked source files, total bytes processed

### Deliverables
- CLI subcommand registration
- Read from scrape-state.json, index stats, daemon PID file
- Human-readable table output
- --json flag for structured output

### References
- CLI reference: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/cli-reference.md

title: Phase 5: SQLite Format Support & Extended Agents
description:
Add SQLite format parser for Cursor, Windsurf, and similar agents.

## Deliverables

### SQLite Format Parser
- Add sqlite format handler to plugin framework (rusqlite read-only)
- Parse cursorDiskKV table structure (composerData + bubbleId keys)
- Handle large databases (25GB+) with read-only mode and page cache limits
- Files: src/parser/sqlite.rs

### Cursor Plugin
- Bundle cursor.toml plugin definition
- Extract sessions from state.vscdb (global + workspace)
- Handle composerData:<composerId> and bubbleId:<composerId>:<bubbleId> keys
- Files: plugins/cursor.toml

### Windsurf Plugin
- Bundle windsurf.toml plugin definition
- Handle 20-conversation hard limit (scrape-early-or-lose-data warning)
- Detect protobuf format in newer versions
- Files: plugins/windsurf.toml

### Community Plugin Examples
- Create plugins/examples/ directory with documented template
- Example: custom-agent.toml with all configuration options annotated

### Git Auto-Commit Integration
- Optionally commit new normalized sessions to git on scrape
- Config: [scrape] git_auto_commit = false
- Files: src/scrape/git_commit.rs

## Testing
- Unit tests with sample state.vscdb fixtures
- Test truncation detection (file shrinks between scrapes)
- Test large database handling (PRAGMA cache_size enforcement)
- Verify Windsurf overwrite detection triggers full rescan

## Acceptance Criteria
- `agentscribe scrape` correctly parses Cursor state.vscdb files
- Windsurf plugin warns about 20-conversation limit in status output
- Custom plugin TOML with format = "sqlite" works without code changes
- Git auto-commit creates one commit per scrape with session count in message
